content_html:
Matching Engine Algorithm Performance Challenge

A reproducible benchmark for limit-order-book matching engine algorithms: one deterministic workload, one byte-identical correctness oracle, identical work for every engine. Published with "The World's Fastest Matching Engine Algorithm" (Flash One Technologies, 2026).1

The survey aims to cover every publicly available FIFO matching implementation without duplicates; to request coverage of an engine, contact contact@flash1.com.

Results

The harness has been run against 247 distinct matching engines — every common FIFO book architecture, 20+ source languages. 160 reproduce the byte-identical consensus (113 of them only after a documented correctness fix at their pinned commit; three have since merged that fix upstream and now conform as shipped, so 110 still carry ours) and are listed in CONSENSUS_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md; the other 87 diverge, cannot finish their slowest scenario within the message budget, or crash (NON_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md).

The consensus oracle is also a bug-finder: across the 247 engines tested, wherever it surfaced a reportable finding we drafted a fix and reported it, respectfully: 181 GitHub issues filed upstream for +250 distinct findings — 18 already fixed by their maintainers, none declined (RESOLVED_FINDINGS.md). The full decomposition and per-engine one-line findings are in CORRECTNESS_FINDINGS.md; the industry-authored subset is broken out in INDUSTRY_AUTHORED_ENGINES.md.

No number or finding in any document in this repo ranks or judges an author's engineering quality — the harness merely reports what each pinned commit does.

The top 10 by worst-case throughput on seed 23 — each engine's lowest of the five scenarios (seed 23, Graviton4 / Neoverse-V2, -O3 -march=native; median of 10 trials). FlashOne is the harness publisher's algorithm, shown as a reference; it stands as a target to beat on fully public, audited work. Every verdict in these rosters describes each engine at its pinned commit in SNAPSHOTS.md — a reproducible snapshot, not a claim about a project's current code or its authors' engineering quality.

‡ = authored by a professional trading-industry engineer — a personal side project with no commercial intent, not their employer's work, except where the Notes explicitly label an official vendor/org repo. Affiliations are as the authors state them publicly. · ★ = repository has 50+ GitHub stars. Published figure = the project's own advertised number under its own workload, hardware, and definition — shown as context, not directly comparable to this harness's worst-case. The industry-authored subset is broken out in INDUSTRY_AUTHORED_ENGINES.md.

Every conforming engine, classified by the data structure it uses to order price levels — the core algorithmic choice in a FIFO matcher. The figure is the best worst-case throughput any engine reached with that structure: an upper bound, since language and implementation confound a pure structural comparison (every leader is C, C++, or Rust).

Latency under burst load

Throughput is what an exchange measures internally; what a trader experiences when the market moves is latency, and latency under load is a property of headroom. This table stress-tests five high-throughput conforming engines, each in its own weakest scenario, at offered loads in the documented microburst range (5–12 M msg/s).2 All values are nanoseconds, P50 / P99.

Engine Weakest scenario 5 M/s 8 M/s 12 M/s
FlashOne normal 354 / 534 363 / 568 383 / 623
cpp-orderbook swing-25 363 / 2,190 457 / 3,309 21,400,000 / 33,100,000 †
CppTrader normal 387 / 1,984 658 / 3,606 23,200,000 / 39,900,000 †
Kautenja normal 428 / 3,070 4,740,000 / 17,500,000 † 45,400,000 / 91,500,000 †
asthamishra flash-crash 496 / 3,153 42,400,000 / 59,100,000 † 90,600,000 / 139,000,000 †

† ρ > 1 — the offered load is past the engine's sustainable throughput in that scenario. The queue grows without bound; therefore, in that case, the figure is not a convergent latency: it is the median delay accrued over the fixed ~2 M-message burst and rises with burst length.

The engines shown are a representative high-throughput group (worst-case ceilings in the ≈ 6–8 M/s band), chosen so the tested offered loads bracket their saturation points and isolate the objective latency effect of matcher saturation - not a judgment or verdict on any individual engine.

Worst-case stress test. P50 / P99 are measured from each message's scheduled arrival, open-loop at the stated offered rate, coordinated-omission-free (the queueing delay a slow matcher imposes is never hidden). Every engine eventually diverges as ρ → 1. FlashOne's latency knee sits at ≈ 30 M/s (near-edge P50 ≈ 2.1 µs), and it sustains ≈ 31 M/s.

Quick start

git clone <repo-url> matching-engine-benchmark
cd matching-engine-benchmark
make                                            # builds ./harness and ./generator
scripts/build_baselines.sh liquibook quantcup   # two baselines (audit checks one against the other)
./harness --baseline liquibook --scenario normal --mode perf
./harness --baseline liquibook --scenario normal --mode audit

scripts/build_baselines.sh all builds all three baselines (Exchange-core also needs a JDK 11 and Maven; see Requirements).

To benchmark your own engine algorithm, implement the C ABI in api/matching_engine_api.h, build it as a .so, and:

./harness --engine /path/to/your_engine.so --scenario normal --mode perf

See docs/INTEGRATION.md.

On a correctness failure, export the candidate's canonical report stream and localize the first differing sequence without changing the consensus reference:

./harness --engine /path/to/your_engine.so --scenario normal --mode perf \
  --write-canonical-output /tmp/candidate.txt
scripts/explain_divergence.py reference/canonical_output.txt.gz /tmp/candidate.txt \
  --json-output /tmp/divergence.json

Compare engines on your own platform:

scripts/build_baselines.sh all
scripts/run_challenge.py --compare liquibook quantcup exchange_core   # all 5 + worst-case ordering

Why worst-case throughput

A matching engine is the serial heart of an exchange: per-symbol price–time matching is strictly sequential, so its single-core throughput caps the whole venue.3 Order flow arrives in microbursts — Deutsche Börse's T7 measurements show inbound flow peaking in the millions of messages a second at the gateway while only a few hundred thousand a second reach matching2 — so a burst queues behind the matcher, and that queue, not the average speed, becomes the worst-case delay in the moments that matter most.4 The feedback loop tightens over time: as the exchange gets faster, customers react faster and "send us more transactions in sharper peaks."2

When the matcher falls behind, market makers are picked off at stale prices, lose money, and quote wider and thinner — costs that land on investors.56 The benchmark's definitional result is therefore worst-case throughput: the lowest of an engine's five scenario results, with the scenario that produces it.

For the plain-language version of this argument and the market-quality evidence, see the paper's §2.1

Methodology in brief

Scope. A single matching engine on one symbol, driven one message at a time through ~2.0M new/cancel/modify messages (an optional engine_on_batch entry point amortises the boundary cost for engines behind a language runtime — the per-message processing is unchanged). The comparison tables report an engine's batched figure only where it improves worst-case throughput by a meaningful margin (> 0.1 M/s); where batching is neutral or slower — as it measured for CPython — the one-at-a-time figure stands. The engine emits its own report stream (OrderAck / Trade / CancelAck / ModifyAck / CancelReject / ModifyReject) over an inter-thread transport drained on an adjacent core, so the measured throughput includes the matcher-to-publisher hand-off a real exchange pays. Multi-core scaling, networking, and risk checks are out of scope.

Workload. A deterministic, realistically shaped equity order flow: GBM mid-price, power-law depth,7 and a 95% cancel8 / 15% IOC9 / 20% modify lifecycle — the paper benchmark's construction.1 The canonical seed is 23; the harness hand-rolls every distribution so a seed reproduces the same workload across compilers (docs/METHODOLOGY.md records why the paper's seed 12345 realises differently here).

Correctness. The engine's full report output is hashed (SHA-256) against reference/correctness_hash.txt — the byte-identical consensus first established from three independent engines (so it favours no single design) and since reproduced by the whole conforming field.

A full challenge is 10 perf runs + 1 audit run per scenario (scripts/run_challenge.py); VALID only if every perf hash and the audit pass. The audit replays the workload through a baseline engine and probes order-book state at random points; every run probes the same points, so an engine cannot tell a measured run from an audited one (docs/ANTI_CHEAT.md). By default all five scenarios run and the engine's worst case is reported (--scenario narrows; --compare orders by worst case).

Pre-run conformance gate. Before any timed run, each engine passes a battery of 34 hand-crafted edge cases, reverse-engineered from latent bugs found across the survey, plus a book-state audit (best_bid / best_ask / depth checked against the consensus) that catches a stale or phantom book state that self-heals before any trade and so escapes the report hash. It tests only hard invariants every correct book must satisfy, never engine-specific conventions (docs/CONFORMANCE.md).

The rosters

  • CONSENSUS_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md — the 160 conforming engines (byte-identical on output and book state across 100 random seeds, 1 billion+ order messages each, plus the conformance gate). as shipped = conforms unmodified; with fix = conforms after the minimal documented patch.
  • NON_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md — the 87 whose output diverges, or that cannot finish their worst scenario within budget. Non-conforming describes the output against the consensus, not engineering quality.
  • INDUSTRY_AUTHORED_ENGINES.md — the 52 industry-authored engines, with the official/production repos and the production DEX matchers broken out.
  • CORRECTNESS_FINDINGS.md / RESOLVED_FINDINGS.md — per-engine findings and the fixes already merged upstream.

Requirements

  • Linux, GCC 14+ or Clang 16+ (C++20), CMake 3.16+, Boost headers.
  • scripts/build_baselines.sh additionally needs git; for the Exchange-core baseline it needs a JDK 11 and Maven.
  • Python 3.8+ for the wrapper scripts.
  • The additional_references/ example adapters pull their own toolchains (Rust, Go, a JDK 11+ for the Java/JNI adapters, Node for the TypeScript/JavaScript adapters; the Python adapters embed CPython) — each build.sh auto-installs the pinned toolchain (rustup / go.dev / etc.) if it is absent, which requires curl and network access. The jxm35 adapter additionally needs a C++23 compiler and libfmt (libfmt-dev).

FAQ

Q. Why did you build this?

A. To give the community a neutral, reproducible way to measure a matching engine's throughput and verify its correctness on identical work — something that did not exist in the open. The workload, the baseline adapters, and the byte-identical reference hashes are all public, so anyone can run any engine against the same work and the same correctness oracle, on their own hardware. That oracle — the byte-identical consensus that independent open-source engines reproduce, first anchored by three of them — has already surfaced real bugs in several open-source matching engines, including a latent one in a codebase nearly nine years old.

The same openness applies to our own claim: the paper's title — "The World's Fastest Matching Engine Algorithm" — is deliberately falsifiable. Beat FlashOne's published numbers on the same work and the title is wrong — a test we are openly inviting. If you reach comparable or higher numbers with your own design, or you think the method is unfair, we would love to hear from you.

Q. Isn't this a self-serving benchmark — any conflict of interest in writing your own benchmark?

A. The test is built in a way that our judgment does not enter it. The correctness reference is the byte-identical consensus that independent open-source engines reproduce — first established from three of them (Liquibook10, QuantCup11, Exchange-core12) and since reproduced across the whole conforming field — not our say-so; the workload generator, the adapters, and the reference hashes are public and deterministic, so anyone can independently verify their internal workings. We do not host a leaderboard or rank submissions.

Q. I only want to check that my engine is correct — can I ignore the throughput number?

A. Yes. Run --mode audit: it verifies the full report-stream hash against the byte-identical consensus and audits the live order book at random points. A Verdict: VALID means your engine reproduces the consensus output and maintains a real book, regardless of speed — for many users that correctness signal is the more valuable half.

Q. Isn't a fast matching engine easy to build?

A. This question conflates implementing a fast matcher with inveting one. Implementing a fast one is easy — a textbook tree-of-lists matcher is straightforward, and most trading-industry engineers have written one. Inventing a fast one that is several times faster on the same hardware is not, and the intuition that conflates the two survives only because the gap has never been plotted.

Plotted, it is stark: all 159 conforming open-source engines top out at 8.19 M/s — the band that language choice, cache tuning, and an OS tweak buy — and nothing occupies the ~25 M/s above it. FlashOne reaches 33.20 M/s not as a faster implementation of the same design but as a different algorithm (the structures are disclosed in the paper); in HFT a 5–10% gain is already substantial, so a 4× margin is not what implementation tweaks accumulate to.

Q. Isn't matcher latency an insignificant part of overall wire-to-wire latency?

A. Yes, on average. But the matcher's throughput headroom — not its average latency — is what shapes the P99 latency curve, and that tail is exactly what a burst exposes. Deutsche Börse (one of Europe's largest exchange operators) makes the point itself: "Our customers are constantly getting faster and send us more transactions in sharper peaks → requires higher throughput on our side."2

Q. There are a lot of variables the challenge doesn't consider — networking, exchange-specific or per-jurisdiction rules, and the like.

A. By design. The challenge is built to compare matching engine algorithms apples-to-apples, not whole matching engines. A deployed engine also carries networking, exchange-specific rules, and per-jurisdiction regulatory requirements that differ enormously from venue to venue — so comparing full engines across those differences would mean far less. Holding those layers out of scope is exactly what isolates the algorithm so two of them can be measured on identical work.

MIT — see LICENSE. The three baseline engines are fetched from their own upstream repositories under their own licenses; docs/PATCHES.md records every modification made to build them.

About Flash One Technologies

We're a team of passionate Math & CS researchers advancing the technological frontier with research-level mathematics. Flash One is a patent IP licensing business, not a matching-engine vendor. We do not offer full matching-engine products. For inquiries, please get in touch with contact@flash1.com.

References

The market-microstructure claims in Why worst-case throughput and the workload-calibration constants in Methodology in brief trace to the benchmark's companion paper and the primary literature it draws on. Full bibliographic detail (54 references) is in the paper; the notes below cite the specific sources behind each claim.

This table stress-tests five high-throughput conforming engines, each in its own weakest scenario, at offered loads in the documented microburst range (5–12 M msg/s).2 All values are nanoseconds, P50 / P99. Engine Weakest scenario 5 M/s 8 M/s 12 M/s FlashOne normal 354 / 534 363 / 568 383 / 623 cpp-orderbook swing-25 363 / 2,190 457 / 3,309 21,400,000 / 33,100,000 † CppTrader normal 387 / 1,984 658 / 3,606 23,200,000 / 39,900,000 † Kautenja normal 428 / 3,070 4,740,000 / 17,500,000 † 45,400,000 / 91,500,000 † asthamishra flash-crash 496 / 3,153 42,400,000 / 59,100,000 † 90,600,000 / 139,000,000 † † ρ > 1 — the offered load is past the engine's sustainable throughput in that scenario. The queue grows without bound; therefore, in that case, the figure is not a convergent latency: it is the median delay accrued over the fixed ~2 M-message burst and rises with burst length. The engines shown are a representative high-throughput group (worst-case ceilings in the ≈ 6–8 M/s band), chosen so the tested offered loads bracket their saturation points and isolate the objective latency effect of matcher saturation - not a judgment or verdict on any individual engine. Worst-case stress test. P50 / P99 are measured from each message's scheduled arrival, open-loop at the stated offered rate, coordinated-omission-free (the queueing delay a slow matcher imposes is never hidden). Every engine eventually diverges as ρ → 1. FlashOne's latency knee sits at ≈ 30 M/s (near-edge P50 ≈ 2.1 µs), and it sustains ≈ 31 M/s. Quick start git clone <repo-url> matching-engine-benchmark cd matching-engine-benchmark make # builds ./harness and ./generator scripts/build_baselines.sh liquibook quantcup # two baselines (audit checks one against the other) ./harness --baseline liquibook --scenario normal --mode perf ./harness --baseline liquibook --scenario normal --mode audit scripts/build_baselines.sh all builds all three baselines (Exchange-core also needs a JDK 11 and Maven; see Requirements). To benchmark your own engine algorithm, implement the C ABI in api/matching_engine_api.h, build it as a .so, and: ./harness --engine /path/to/your_engine.so --scenario normal --mode perf See docs/INTEGRATION.md. On a correctness failure, export the candidate's canonical report stream and localize the first differing sequence without changing the consensus reference: ./harness --engine /path/to/your_engine.so --scenario normal --mode perf \ --write-canonical-output /tmp/candidate.txt scripts/explain_divergence.py reference/canonical_output.txt.gz /tmp/candidate.txt \ --json-output /tmp/divergence.json Compare engines on your own platform: scripts/build_baselines.sh all scripts/run_challenge.py --compare liquibook quantcup exchange_core # all 5 + worst-case ordering Why worst-case throughput A matching engine is the serial heart of an exchange: per-symbol price–time matching is strictly sequential, so its single-core throughput caps the whole venue.3 Order flow arrives in microbursts — Deutsche Börse's T7 measurements show inbound flow peaking in the millions of messages a second at the gateway while only a few hundred thousand a second reach matching2 — so a burst queues behind the matcher, and that queue, not the average speed, becomes the worst-case delay in the moments that matter most.4 The feedback loop tightens over time: as the exchange gets faster, customers react faster and "send us more transactions in sharper peaks."2 When the matcher falls behind, market makers are picked off at stale prices, lose money, and quote wider and thinner — costs that land on investors.56 The benchmark's definitional result is therefore worst-case throughput: the lowest of an engine's five scenario results, with the scenario that produces it. For the plain-language version of this argument and the market-quality evidence, see the paper's §2.1 Methodology in brief Scope. A single matching engine on one symbol, driven one message at a time through ~2.0M new/cancel/modify messages (an optional engine_on_batch entry point amortises the boundary cost for engines behind a language runtime — the per-message processing is unchanged). The comparison tables report an engine's batched figure only where it improves worst-case throughput by a meaningful margin (> 0.1 M/s); where batching is neutral or slower — as it measured for CPython — the one-at-a-time figure stands. The engine emits its own report stream (OrderAck / Trade / CancelAck / ModifyAck / CancelReject / ModifyReject) over an inter-thread transport drained on an adjacent core, so the measured throughput includes the matcher-to-publisher hand-off a real exchange pays. Multi-core scaling, networking, and risk checks are out of scope. Workload. A deterministic, realistically shaped equity order flow: GBM mid-price, power-law depth,7 and a 95% cancel8 / 15% IOC9 / 20% modify lifecycle — the paper benchmark's construction.1 The canonical seed is 23; the harness hand-rolls every distribution so a seed reproduces the same workload across compilers (docs/METHODOLOGY.md records why the paper's seed 12345 realises differently here). Correctness. The engine's full report output is hashed (SHA-256) against reference/correctness_hash.txt — the byte-identical consensus first established from three independent engines (so it favours no single design) and since reproduced by the whole conforming field. A full challenge is 10 perf runs + 1 audit run per scenario (scripts/run_challenge.py); VALID only if every perf hash and the audit pass. The audit replays the workload through a baseline engine and probes order-book state at random points; every run probes the same points, so an engine cannot tell a measured run from an audited one (docs/ANTI_CHEAT.md). By default all five scenarios run and the engine's worst case is reported (--scenario narrows; --compare orders by worst case). Pre-run conformance gate. Before any timed run, each engine passes a battery of 34 hand-crafted edge cases, reverse-engineered from latent bugs found across the survey, plus a book-state audit (best_bid / best_ask / depth checked against the consensus) that catches a stale or phantom book state that self-heals before any trade and so escapes the report hash. It tests only hard invariants every correct book must satisfy, never engine-specific conventions (docs/CONFORMANCE.md). The rosters CONSENSUS_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md — the 160 conforming engines (byte-identical on output and book state across 100 random seeds, 1 billion+ order messages each, plus the conformance gate). as shipped = conforms unmodified; with fix = conforms after the minimal documented patch. NON_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md — the 87 whose output diverges, or that cannot finish their worst scenario within budget. Non-conforming describes the output against the consensus, not engineering quality. INDUSTRY_AUTHORED_ENGINES.md — the 52 industry-authored engines, with the official/production repos and the production DEX matchers broken out. CORRECTNESS_FINDINGS.md / RESOLVED_FINDINGS.md — per-engine findings and the fixes already merged upstream. Repository layout api/ the C ABI an engine implements workload/ the deterministic workload generator src/ the harness — runner, transport, correctness, audit, platform adapters/ the three baseline-engine adapters (Liquibook, QuantCup, Exchange-core) additional_references/ 40 worked adapter examples for third-party engines — the permissively-licensed subset of the ~134 adapted across the survey (20+ languages incl. C++/Rust/Go/Java/Python/TypeScript/JavaScript/C#/Zig/C/Haskell/OCaml/Scala/Kotlin/Ruby); the rest are held data-only patches/ source patches applied to baseline engines (QuantCup) reference/ the published canonical report output and its hash scripts/ build_baselines.sh, run_challenge.py, compare_results.py docs/ METHODOLOGY, INTEGRATION, ANTI_CHEAT, CONFORMANCE, PATCHES tests/ a SHA-256 self-test and three anti-cheat cheat adapters CORRECTNESS_FINDINGS.md per-engine correctness verdict + filed-issue link, full audited set CONSENSUS_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md the conforming roster + worst-case throughput NON_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md the non-conforming roster RESOLVED_FINDINGS.md findings since fixed upstream (CppTrader, geseq, matchingo, cheetah, OrderBook-rs, matchina, rinok, pyorderbook, dx1ngy) SNAPSHOTS.md the pinned upstream commit for every audited engine Requirements Linux, GCC 14+ or Clang 16+ (C++20), CMake 3.16+, Boost headers. scripts/build_baselines.sh additionally needs git; for the Exchange-core baseline it needs a JDK 11 and Maven. Python 3.8+ for the wrapper scripts. The additional_references/ example adapters pull their own toolchains (Rust, Go, a JDK 11+ for the Java/JNI adapters, Node for the TypeScript/JavaScript adapters; the Python adapters embed CPython) — each build.sh auto-installs the pinned toolchain (rustup / go.dev / etc.) if it is absent, which requires curl and network access. The jxm35 adapter additionally needs a C++23 compiler and libfmt (libfmt-dev). FAQ Q. Why did you build this? A. To give the community a neutral, reproducible way to measure a matching engine's throughput and verify its correctness on identical work — something that did not exist in the open. The workload, the baseline adapters, and the byte-identical reference hashes are all public, so anyone can run any engine against the same work and the same correctness oracle, on their own hardware. That oracle — the byte-identical consensus that independent open-source engines reproduce, first anchored by three of them — has already surfaced real bugs in several open-source matching engines, including a latent one in a codebase nearly nine years old. The same openness applies to our own claim: the paper's title — "The World's Fastest Matching Engine Algorithm" — is deliberately falsifiable. Beat FlashOne's published numbers on the same work and the title is wrong — a test we are openly inviting. If you reach comparable or higher numbers with your own design, or you think the method is unfair, we would love to hear from you. Q. Isn't this a self-serving benchmark — any conflict of interest in writing your own benchmark? A. The test is built in a way that our judgment does not enter it. The correctness reference is the byte-identical consensus that independent open-source engines reproduce — first established from three of them (Liquibook10, QuantCup11, Exchange-core12) and since reproduced across the whole conforming field — not our say-so; the workload generator, the adapters, and the reference hashes are public and deterministic, so anyone can independently verify their internal workings. We do not host a leaderboard or rank submissions. Q. I only want to check that my engine is correct — can I ignore the throughput number? A. Yes. Run --mode audit: it verifies the full report-stream hash against the byte-identical consensus and audits the live order book at random points. A Verdict: VALID means your engine reproduces the consensus output and maintains a real book, regardless of speed — for many users that correctness signal is the more valuable half. Q. Isn't a fast matching engine easy to build? A. This question conflates implementing a fast matcher with inveting one. Implementing a fast one is easy — a textbook tree-of-lists matcher is straightforward, and most trading-industry engineers have written one. Inventing a fast one that is several times faster on the same hardware is not, and the intuition that conflates the two survives only because the gap has never been plotted. Plotted, it is stark: all 159 conforming open-source engines top out at 8.19 M/s — the band that language choice, cache tuning, and an OS tweak buy — and nothing occupies the ~25 M/s above it. FlashOne reaches 33.20 M/s not as a faster implementation of the same design but as a different algorithm (the structures are disclosed in the paper); in HFT a 5–10% gain is already substantial, so a 4× margin is not what implementation tweaks accumulate to. Q. Isn't matcher latency an insignificant part of overall wire-to-wire latency? A. Yes, on average. But the matcher's throughput headroom — not its average latency — is what shapes the P99 latency curve, and that tail is exactly what a burst exposes. Deutsche Börse (one of Europe's largest exchange operators) makes the point itself: "Our customers are constantly getting faster and send us more transactions in sharper peaks → requires higher throughput on our side."2 Q. There are a lot of variables the challenge doesn't consider — networking, exchange-specific or per-jurisdiction rules, and the like. A. By design. The challenge is built to compare matching engine algorithms apples-to-apples, not whole matching engines. A deployed engine also carries networking, exchange-specific rules, and per-jurisdiction regulatory requirements that differ enormously from venue to venue — so comparing full engines across those differences would mean far less. Holding those layers out of scope is exactly what isolates the algorithm so two of them can be measured on identical work. License MIT — see LICENSE. The three baseline engines are fetched from their own upstream repositories under their own licenses; docs/PATCHES.md records every modification made to build them. Flash One is a patent IP licensing business, not a matching-engine vendor. We do not offer full matching-engine products. For inquiries, please get in touch with contact@flash1.com. sentences.en: Why worst-case throughput
sentences.zh: 为什么最坏情况吞吐量

blocks.html_en: A matching engine is the serial heart of an exchange: per-symbol price–time matching is strictly sequential, so its single-core throughput caps the whole venue.3 Order flow arrives in microbursts — Deutsche Börse's T7 measurements show inbound flow peaking in the millions of messages a second at the gateway while only a few hundred thousand a second reach matching2 — so a burst queues behind the matcher, and that queue, not the average speed, becomes the worst-case delay in the moments that matter most.4 The feedback loop tightens over time: as the exchange gets faster, customers react faster and "send us more transactions in sharper peaks."2

sentences.en: A matching engine is the serial heart of an exchange: per-symbol price–time matching is strictly sequential, so its single-core throughput caps the whole venue.3 Order flow arrives in microbursts — Deutsche Börse's T7 measurements show inbound flow peaking in the millions of messages a second at the gateway while only a few hundred thousand a second reach matching2 — so a burst queues behind the matcher, and that queue, not the average speed, becomes the worst-case delay in the moments that matter most.4 The feedback loop tightens over time: as the exchange gets faster, customers react faster and "send us more transactions in sharper peaks."2
sentences.zh: 匹配引擎是交易所的串行核心：每个交易品种的价格-时间匹配是严格顺序的，因此其单核吞吐量限制了整个场地。3 订单流以微爆发的形式到达 — 德意志交易所的 T7 测量显示网关处每秒数百万条消息的入站流量达到峰值，而每秒只有几十万条消息到达匹配2 — 因此匹配器后面的突发队列，而该队列（而不是平均速度）成为重要时刻的最坏情况延迟大多数。4反馈循环随着时间的推移而收紧：随着交易速度加快，客户反应更快，并“在更高峰时向我们发送更多交易。”2

blocks.html_en: When the matcher falls behind, market makers are picked off at stale prices, lose money, and quote wider and thinner — costs that land on investors.56 The benchmark's definitional result is therefore worst-case throughput: the lowest of an engine's five scenario results, with the scenario that produces it.

sentences.en: When the matcher falls behind, market makers are picked off at stale prices, lose money, and quote wider and thinner — costs that land on investors.56 The benchmark's definitional result is therefore worst-case throughput: the lowest of an engine's five scenario results, with the scenario that produces it.
sentences.zh: 当匹配者落后时，做市商就会以过时的价格被淘汰，赔钱，报价范围更广、更薄——成本落在投资者身上。56因此，基准的定义结果是最坏情况的吞吐量：引擎的五种情况结果中最低的一个，以及产生该结果的情况。

sentences.zh: 方法论简介

blocks.html_en: Scope. A single matching engine on one symbol, driven one message at a time through ~2.0M new/cancel/modify messages (an optional engine_on_batch entry point amortises the boundary cost for engines behind a language runtime — the per-message processing is unchanged). The comparison tables report an engine's batched figure only where it improves worst-case throughput by a meaningful margin (> 0.1 M/s); where batching is neutral or slower — as it measured for CPython — the one-at-a-time figure stands. The engine emits its own report stream (OrderAck / Trade / CancelAck / ModifyAck / CancelReject / ModifyReject) over an inter-thread transport drained on an adjacent core, so the measured throughput includes the matcher-to-publisher hand-off a real exchange pays. Multi-core scaling, networking, and risk checks are out of scope.

sentences.en: Scope.
sentences.zh: 范围。

sentences.en: A single matching engine on one symbol, driven one message at a time through ~2.0M new/cancel/modify messages (an optional engine_on_batch entry point amortises the boundary cost for engines behind a language runtime — the per-message processing is unchanged).
sentences.zh: 一个符号上的单个匹配引擎，通过约 200 万条新/取消/修改消息一次驱动一条消息（可选的 engine_on_batch 入口点摊销语言运行时后面的引擎的边界成本 - 每条消息的处理不变）。

sentences.en: The comparison tables report an engine's batched figure only where it improves worst-case throughput by a meaningful margin (> 0.1 M/s); where batching is neutral or slower — as it measured for CPython — the one-at-a-time figure stands.
sentences.zh: 比较表仅报告引擎在最坏情况下吞吐量显着提高（> 0.1 M/s）的情况下的批处理数据；如果批处理是中性的或较慢的（正如它对 CPython 的测量结果），则一次一个的数字就成立。

sentences.en: The engine emits its own report stream (OrderAck / Trade / CancelAck / ModifyAck / CancelReject / ModifyReject) over an inter-thread transport drained on an adjacent core, so the measured throughput includes the matcher-to-publisher hand-off a real exchange pays.
sentences.zh: 引擎通过相邻核心上的线程间传输发出自己的报告流（OrderAck / Trade / CancelAck / ModifyAck / CancelReject / ModifyReject），因此测量的吞吐量包括真实交易所支付的匹配器到发布者的切换。

sentences.en: Multi-core scaling, networking, and risk checks are out of scope.
sentences.zh: 多核扩展、网络和风险检查超出了范围。

blocks.html_en: Workload. A deterministic, realistically shaped equity order flow: GBM mid-price, power-law depth,7 and a 95% cancel8 / 15% IOC9 / 20% modify lifecycle — the paper benchmark's construction.1 The canonical seed is 23; the harness hand-rolls every distribution so a seed reproduces the same workload across compilers (docs/METHODOLOGY.md records why the paper's seed 12345 realises differently here).

sentences.en: A deterministic, realistically shaped equity order flow: GBM mid-price, power-law depth,7 and a 95% cancel8 / 15% IOC9 / 20% modify lifecycle — the paper benchmark's construction.1 The canonical seed is 23; the harness hand-rolls every distribution so a seed reproduces the same workload across compilers (docs/METHODOLOGY.md records why the paper's seed 12345 realises differently here).
sentences.zh: 确定性、现实形状的股权订单流：GBM 中间价格、幂律深度 7 和 95% 取消 8 / 15% IOC9 / 20% 修改生命周期 — 论文基准的构建。1 规范种子为 23；该工具会手动滚动每个发行版，以便种子在编译器之间重现相同的工作负载（docs/METHODOLOGY.md 记录了本文的种子 12345 在这里实现不同的原因）。

sentences.en: The engine's full report output is hashed (SHA-256) against reference/correctness_hash.txt — the byte-identical consensus first established from three independent engines (so it favours no single design) and since reproduced by the whole conforming field.
sentences.zh: 引擎的完整报告输出根据reference/ Correctness_hash.txt进行散列（SHA-256）——字节相同的共识首先从三个独立的引擎建立（因此它不支持单一设计），并由整个一致领域复制。

blocks.html_en: A full challenge is 10 perf runs + 1 audit run per scenario (scripts/run_challenge.py); VALID only if every perf hash and the audit pass. The audit replays the workload through a baseline engine and probes order-book state at random points; every run probes the same points, so an engine cannot tell a measured run from an audited one (docs/ANTI_CHEAT.md). By default all five scenarios run and the engine's worst case is reported (--scenario narrows; --compare orders by worst case).

sentences.en: The audit replays the workload through a baseline engine and probes order-book state at random points; every run probes the same points, so an engine cannot tell a measured run from an audited one (docs/ANTI_CHEAT.md).

blocks.html_en: Pre-run conformance gate. Before any timed run, each engine passes a battery of 34 hand-crafted edge cases, reverse-engineered from latent bugs found across the survey, plus a book-state audit (best_bid / best_ask / depth checked against the consensus) that catches a stale or phantom book state that self-heals before any trade and so escapes the report hash. It tests only hard invariants every correct book must satisfy, never engine-specific conventions (docs/CONFORMANCE.md).

sentences.en: Pre-run conformance gate.
sentences.zh: 预运行一致性门。

sentences.en: Before any timed run, each engine passes a battery of 34 hand-crafted edge cases, reverse-engineered from latent bugs found across the survey, plus a book-state audit (best_bid / best_ask / depth checked against the consensus) that catches a stale or phantom book state that self-heals before any trade and so escapes the report hash.
sentences.zh: 在任何定时运行之前，每个引擎都会通过一组由 34 个手工制作的边缘情况组成的电池，这些边缘情况是根据调查中发现的潜在错误进行逆向工程的，再加上账本状态审核（最佳出价/最佳卖价/深度与共识进行检查），以捕获陈旧或虚幻的账本状态，该状态会在任何交易之前自我修复，从而逃脱报告哈希。

blocks.html_en: Q. Why did you build this?

sentences.en: Q.
sentences.zh: 问。

sentences.en: Why did you build this?
sentences.zh: 你为什么建造这个？

blocks.html_en: A. To give the community a neutral, reproducible way to measure a matching engine's throughput and verify its correctness on identical work — something that did not exist in the open. The workload, the baseline adapters, and the byte-identical reference hashes are all public, so anyone can run any engine against the same work and the same correctness oracle, on their own hardware. That oracle — the byte-identical consensus that independent open-source engines reproduce, first anchored by three of them — has already surfaced real bugs in several open-source matching engines, including a latent one in a codebase nearly nine years old.

sentences.en: A.
sentences.zh: 一个。

sentences.en: To give the community a neutral, reproducible way to measure a matching engine's throughput and verify its correctness on identical work — something that did not exist in the open.
sentences.zh: 为社区提供一种中立、可重复的方式来测量匹配引擎的吞吐量并验证其在相同工作上的正确性——这是公开存在的。

sentences.en: The workload, the baseline adapters, and the byte-identical reference hashes are all public, so anyone can run any engine against the same work and the same correctness oracle, on their own hardware.

sentences.en: That oracle — the byte-identical consensus that independent open-source engines reproduce, first anchored by three of them — has already surfaced real bugs in several open-source matching engines, including a latent one in a codebase nearly nine years old.
sentences.zh: 该预言机——独立开源引擎复制的字节相同共识，首先由其中的三个引擎锚定——已经在几个开源匹配引擎中暴露了真正的错误，其中包括近九年前的代码库中的一个潜在错误。

blocks.html_en: The same openness applies to our own claim: the paper's title — "The World's Fastest Matching Engine Algorithm" — is deliberately falsifiable. Beat FlashOne's published numbers on the same work and the title is wrong — a test we are openly inviting. If you reach comparable or higher numbers with your own design, or you think the method is unfair, we would love to hear from you.

sentences.zh: 同样的开放性也适用于我们自己的主张：论文的标题——“世界上最快的匹配引擎算法”——是故意伪造的。

blocks.html_en: Q. Isn't this a self-serving benchmark — any conflict of interest in writing your own benchmark?

sentences.en: Q.
sentences.zh: 问。

sentences.en: Isn't this a self-serving benchmark — any conflict of interest in writing your own benchmark?
sentences.zh: 这难道不是一个自私的基准——编写自己的基准有任何利益冲突吗？

blocks.html_en: A. The test is built in a way that our judgment does not enter it. The correctness reference is the byte-identical consensus that independent open-source engines reproduce — first established from three of them (Liquibook10, QuantCup11, Exchange-core12) and since reproduced across the whole conforming field — not our say-so; the workload generator, the adapters, and the reference hashes are public and deterministic, so anyone can independently verify their internal workings. We do not host a leaderboard or rank submissions.

sentences.en: A.
sentences.zh: 一个。

sentences.en: The test is built in a way that our judgment does not enter it.
sentences.zh: 测试的构建方式不包括我们的判断。

sentences.en: The correctness reference is the byte-identical consensus that independent open-source engines reproduce — first established from three of them (Liquibook10, QuantCup11, Exchange-core12) and since reproduced across the whole conforming field — not our say-so; the workload generator, the adapters, and the reference hashes are public and deterministic, so anyone can independently verify their internal workings.
sentences.zh: 正确性参考是独立开源引擎复制的字节相同共识——首先由其中三个引擎（Liquibook10、QuantCup11、Exchange-core12）建立，此后在整个一致性领域复制——不是我们说的；工作负载生成器、适配器和参考哈希是公开的和确定性的，因此任何人都可以独立验证其内部工作原理。

sentences.en: We do not host a leaderboard or rank submissions.
sentences.zh: 我们不举办排行榜或排名提交。

sentences.en: Q.

blocks.html_en: A. Yes. Run --mode audit: it verifies the full report-stream hash against the byte-identical consensus and audits the live order book at random points. A Verdict: VALID means your engine reproduces the consensus output and maintains a real book, regardless of speed — for many users that correctness signal is the more valuable half.

sentences.en: A.
sentences.zh: 一个。

sentences.en: Yes.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: Run --mode audit: it verifies the full report-stream hash against the byte-identical consensus and audits the live order book at random points.
sentences.zh: 运行 --mode 审计：它根据字节相同的共识验证完整的报告流哈希，并在随机点审计实时订单簿。

sentences.zh: 结论：有效意味着你的引擎会重现共识输出并维护一本真实的书，无论速度如何——对于许多用户来说，正确性信号是更有价值的一半。

blocks.html_en: Q. Isn't a fast matching engine easy to build?

sentences.en: Q.
sentences.zh: 问。

sentences.en: Isn't a fast matching engine easy to build?
sentences.zh: 快速匹配引擎不是很容易构建吗？

blocks.html_en: A. This question conflates implementing a fast matcher with inveting one. Implementing a fast one is easy — a textbook tree-of-lists matcher is straightforward, and most trading-industry engineers have written one. Inventing a fast one that is several times faster on the same hardware is not, and the intuition that conflates the two survives only because the gap has never been plotted.

sentences.en: A.
sentences.zh: 一个。

sentences.en: This question conflates implementing a fast matcher with inveting one.
sentences.zh: 这个问题将实现快速匹配器与投资匹配器混为一谈。

sentences.en: Implementing a fast one is easy — a textbook tree-of-lists matcher is straightforward, and most trading-industry engineers have written one.
sentences.zh: 实现快速匹配器很容易——教科书上的列表树匹配器很简单，大多数交易行业工程师都编写了一个。

sentences.en: Inventing a fast one that is several times faster on the same hardware is not, and the intuition that conflates the two survives only because the gap has never been plotted.
sentences.zh: 在相同的硬件上发明一种速度快数倍的快速软件并不是一件容易的事情，而将两者混为一谈的直觉之所以得以存在，只是因为这种差距从未被绘制出来。

blocks.html_en: Plotted, it is stark: all 159 conforming open-source engines top out at 8.19 M/s — the band that language choice, cache tuning, and an OS tweak buy — and nothing occupies the ~25 M/s above it. FlashOne reaches 33.20 M/s not as a faster implementation of the same design but as a different algorithm (the structures are disclosed in the paper); in HFT a 5–10% gain is already substantial, so a 4× margin is not what implementation tweaks accumulate to.

sentences.en: Plotted, it is stark: all 159 conforming open-source engines top out at 8.19 M/s — the band that language choice, cache tuning, and an OS tweak buy — and nothing occupies the ~25 M/s above it.
sentences.zh: 绘制出来的结果很明显：所有 159 个符合标准的开源引擎的最高速度为 8.19 M/s——这是语言选择、​​缓存调整和操作系统调整所需要的带宽——没有任何东西占据高于它的约 25 M/s。

sentences.en: FlashOne reaches 33.20 M/s not as a faster implementation of the same design but as a different algorithm (the structures are disclosed in the paper); in HFT a 5–10% gain is already substantial, so a 4× margin is not what implementation tweaks accumulate to.
sentences.zh: FlashOne 达到 33.20 M/s，并不是因为相同设计的更快实现，而是因为不同的算法（结构已在论文中公开）；在 HFT 中，5-10% 的增益已经相当可观，因此 4 倍的裕度并不是实现调整累积到的结果。

blocks.html_en: Q. Isn't matcher latency an insignificant part of overall wire-to-wire latency?

sentences.en: Q.
sentences.zh: 问。

sentences.en: Isn't matcher latency an insignificant part of overall wire-to-wire latency?
sentences.zh: 匹配器延迟难道不是整个线对线延迟中微不足道的一部分吗？

blocks.html_en: A. Yes, on average. But the matcher's throughput headroom — not its average latency — is what shapes the P99 latency curve, and that tail is exactly what a burst exposes. Deutsche Börse (one of Europe's largest exchange operators) makes the point itself: "Our customers are constantly getting faster and send us more transactions in sharper peaks → requires higher throughput on our side."2

sentences.en: A.
sentences.zh: 一个。

sentences.en: Yes, on average.
sentences.zh: 是的，平均而言。

sentences.en: But the matcher's throughput headroom — not its average latency — is what shapes the P99 latency curve, and that tail is exactly what a burst exposes.
sentences.zh: 但匹配器的吞吐量余量（而不是其平均延迟）决定了 P99 延迟曲线，而该尾部正是突发所暴露的。

sentences.en: Deutsche Börse (one of Europe's largest exchange operators) makes the point itself: "Our customers are constantly getting faster and send us more transactions in sharper peaks → requires higher throughput on our side."2
sentences.zh: 德意志交易所（欧洲最大的交易所运营商之一）本身就指出了这一点：“我们的客户速度不断加快，并在更高峰时向我们发送更多交易 → 要求我们提高吞吐量。”2

blocks.html_en: Q. There are a lot of variables the challenge doesn't consider — networking, exchange-specific or per-jurisdiction rules, and the like.

sentences.en: Q.
sentences.zh: 问。

sentences.en: There are a lot of variables the challenge doesn't consider — networking, exchange-specific or per-jurisdiction rules, and the like.
sentences.zh: 该挑战赛没有考虑很多变量——网络、特定于交易所或每个司法管辖区的规则等。

blocks.html_en: A. By design. The challenge is built to compare matching engine algorithms apples-to-apples, not whole matching engines. A deployed engine also carries networking, exchange-specific rules, and per-jurisdiction regulatory requirements that differ enormously from venue to venue — so comparing full engines across those differences would mean far less. Holding those layers out of scope is exactly what isolates the algorithm so two of them can be measured on identical work.

sentences.en: A.
sentences.zh: 一个。

sentences.en: By design.
sentences.zh: 通过设计。

sentences.en: The challenge is built to compare matching engine algorithms apples-to-apples, not whole matching engines.
sentences.zh: 该挑战赛旨在比较匹配引擎算法，而不是整个匹配引擎。

sentences.en: A deployed engine also carries networking, exchange-specific rules, and per-jurisdiction regulatory requirements that differ enormously from venue to venue — so comparing full engines across those differences would mean far less.
sentences.zh: 部署的引擎还带有网络、特定于交易所的规则和每个司法管辖区的监管要求，这些要求在不同地点之间存在巨大差异 - 因此，比较具有这些差异的完整引擎的意义要小得多。

sentences.en: Holding those layers out of scope is exactly what isolates the algorithm so two of them can be measured on identical work.
sentences.zh: 将这些层排除在范围之外正是隔离算法的原因，因此可以在相同的工作中测量其中的两个层。

blocks.html_en: • Per-symbol matching is strictly serial under price–time priority, so its single-core throughput is a hard ceiling no surrounding parallelism can lift (Gene M. Amdahl, AFIPS 1967, doi:10.1145/1465482.1465560); once a burst exceeds that ceiling, end-to-end latency is governed by queuing delay rather than compute. Publicly documented per-partition matching ceilings are on the order of ~300,000 orders/s (Deutsche Börse T7; Eurex T7 documentation, 2024). ↩

blocks.html_en: • The latency-arbitrage ("stale-quote sniping") race between liquidity providers cancelling stale quotes and fast traders picking them off: Matteo Aquilina, Eric Budish, and Peter O'Neill, "Quantifying the High-Frequency Trading Arms Race," Quarterly Journal of Economics 137(1), 2022, 493–564 — they estimate eliminating sniping would cut investors' cost of liquidity by up to ~17%. doi:10.1093/qje/qjab032. See also Eric Budish, Peter Cramton, and John Shim, "The High-Frequency Trading Arms Race: Frequent Batch Auctions as a Market Design Response," QJE 130(4), 2015. doi:10.1093/qje/qjv027 ↩

content_html_zh:
匹配引擎算法性能挑战

限价订单簿匹配引擎算法的可重现基准：一个确定性工作负载、一个字节相同的正确性预言机、每个引擎的相同工作。 与“世界上最快的匹配引擎算法”一起发布（Flash One Technologies，2026）。1

该调查旨在涵盖所有公开可用的 FIFO 匹配实现，不重复；要请求对引擎进行保修，请联系 contact@flash1.com。

结果

该工具已针对 247 个不同的匹配引擎运行 - 每个常见的 FIFO 书籍架构、20 多种源语言。 160 个重现了字节相同的共识（其中 113 个仅在其固定提交中记录了正确性修复之后；其中三个已经合并了上游的修复，现在与发货一致，因此 110 个仍然包含我们的共识）并列在 CONSENSUS_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md 中；其他 87 个出现分歧，无法在消息预算内完成最慢的场景，或者崩溃 (NON_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md)。

共识预言机也是一个 bug 发现者：在测试的 247 个引擎中，无论在哪里出现可报告的发现，我们都起草了修复程序并进行了报告：181 个 GitHub 问题向上游提交了超过 250 个不同的发现 - 18 个已经由其维护者修复，没有一个被拒绝（RESOLVED_FINDINGS.md）。 完整的分解和每引擎单行调查结果位于 CORRECTNESS_FINDINGS.md 中；行业创作的子集在 INDUSTRY_AUTHORED_ENGINES.md 中进行了细分。

此存储库中任何文档中的数字或发现都不会排名或判断作者的工程质量 - 该工具仅报告每个固定提交的作用。

种子 23 上最坏情况吞吐量的前 10 名 - 每个引擎是五个场景中最低的（种子 23、Graviton4 / Neoverse-V2、-O3 -march=native；10 次试验的中位数）。 FlashOne是harnesspublisher的算法，仅供参考；它是完全公开、经过审计的工作的目标。 这些名单中的每个判决都描述了 SNAPSHOTS.md 中固定提交的每个引擎 - 一个可重现的快照，而不是关于项目当前代码或其作者的工程质量的声明。

‡ = 由专业贸易行业工程师撰写 - 没有商​​业意图的个人业余项目，不是其雇主的工作，除非注释明确标记了官方供应商/组织存储库。 隶属关系如作者公开声明的那样。 · ★ = 存储库拥有 50 多个 GitHub 星。 发布的数字=项目自己在其自己的工作负载、硬件和定义下公布的数字——显示为上下文，不能直接与该线束的最坏情况进行比较。 行业编写的子集在 INDUSTRY_AUTHORED_ENGINES.md 中进行了细分。

有关所有 160 个符合标准的引擎的完整列表，请参阅 CONSENSUS_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md。

按图书结构划分的吞吐量

每个符合标准的引擎都根据其用于排序价格水平的数据结构进行分类——这是 FIFO 匹配器中的核心算法选择。 该数字是任何引擎在该结构下达到的最佳最坏情况吞吐量：上限，因为语言和实现混淆了纯结构比较（每个领导者都是 C、C++ 或 Rust）。

其他（展开/散列/平面列表） 潘特尔瓦 Go 0.07 5 主要家庭之外的结构；等级上限 0.1 M/s

突发负载下的延迟

吞吐量是交易所内部衡量的指标；当市场变动时，交易者所经历的是延迟，而负载下的延迟是净空的一个属性。 该表对五个高吞吐量一致性引擎进行了压力测试，每个引擎都处于其最弱的场景中，所提供的负载在记录的微突发范围内 (5–12 M msg/s)。2 所有值均为纳秒，P50 / P99。

引擎 最弱的场景 5 M/s 8 M/s 12 M/s
闪一 普通的 354 / 534 363 / 568 383 / 623
CPP订单簿 swing-25 363 / 2,190 457 / 3,309 21,400,000 / 33,100,000 †
Cpp交易者 普通的 387 / 1,984 658 / 3,606 23,200,000 / 39,900,000 †
考滕贾 普通的 428 / 3,070 4,740,000 / 17,500,000 † 45,400,000 / 91,500,000 †
哮喘米什拉 闪崩 496 / 3,153 42,400,000 / 59,100,000 † 90,600,000 / 139,000,000 †

† ρ > 1 — 所提供的负载超过了该场景中发动机的可持续吞吐量。 队列无限增长；因此，在这种情况下，该数字不是收敛延迟：它是固定的约 2 M 消息突发中产生的中值延迟，并随着突发长度而增加。

所示引擎是具有代表性的高吞吐量组（最坏情况上限在 ≈ 6–8 M/s 频段），经过选择，测试提供的负载涵盖其饱和点并隔离匹配器饱和的客观延迟影响 - 不是对任何单个引擎的判断或裁决。

最坏情况压力测试。 P50 / P99 是根据每条消息的预定到达来测量的，以规定的提供速率开环，无协调遗漏（慢速匹配器造成的排队延迟永远不会被隐藏）。 每个引擎最终都会发散为 ρ → 1。 FlashOne 的延迟拐点位于 ≈ 30 M/s（近边缘 P50 ≈ 2.1 µs），并维持 ≈ 31 M/s。

快速启动

git clone <repo-url> matching-engine-benchmark
cd matching-engine-benchmark
make                                            # builds ./harness and ./generator
scripts/build_baselines.sh liquibook quantcup   # two baselines (audit checks one against the other)
./harness --baseline liquibook --scenario normal --mode perf
./harness --baseline liquibook --scenario normal --mode audit

script/build_baselines.sh 全部构建所有三个基线（Exchange-core 还需要 JDK 11 和 Maven；请参阅要求）。

要对您自己的引擎算法进行基准测试，请在 api/matching_engine_api.h 中实现 C ABI，将其构建为 .so，然后：

./harness --engine /path/to/your_engine.so --scenario normal --mode perf

请参阅文档/INTEGRATION.md。

如果正确性失败，导出候选人的规范报告流并本地化第一个不同的序列而不更改共识参考：

./harness --engine /path/to/your_engine.so --scenario normal --mode perf \
  --write-canonical-output /tmp/candidate.txt
scripts/explain_divergence.py reference/canonical_output.txt.gz /tmp/candidate.txt \
  --json-output /tmp/divergence.json

在您自己的平台上比较引擎：

scripts/build_baselines.sh all
scripts/run_challenge.py --compare liquibook quantcup exchange_core   # all 5 + worst-case ordering

为什么最坏情况吞吐量

匹配引擎是交易所的串行核心：每个交易品种的价格-时间匹配是严格顺序的，因此其单核吞吐量限制了整个场地。3 订单流以微爆发的形式到达 — 德意志交易所的 T7 测量显示网关处每秒数百万条消息的入站流量达到峰值，而每秒只有几十万条消息到达匹配2 — 因此匹配器后面的突发队列，而该队列（而不是平均速度）成为重要时刻的最坏情况延迟大多数。4反馈循环随着时间的推移而收紧：随着交易速度加快，客户反应更快，并“在更高峰时向我们发送更多交易。”2

当匹配者落后时，做市商就会以过时的价格被淘汰，赔钱，报价范围更广、更薄——成本落在投资者身上。56因此，基准的定义结果是最坏情况的吞吐量：引擎的五种情况结果中最低的一个，以及产生该结果的情况。

有关该论点的简单语言版本和市场质量证据，请参阅论文的§2.1

方法论简介

范围。 一个符号上的单个匹配引擎，通过约 200 万条新/取消/修改消息一次驱动一条消息（可选的 engine_on_batch 入口点摊销语言运行时后面的引擎的边界成本 - 每条消息的处理不变）。 比较表仅报告引擎在最坏情况下吞吐量显着提高（> 0.1 M/s）的情况下的批处理数据；如果批处理是中性的或较慢的（正如它对 CPython 的测量结果），则一次一个的数字就成立。 引擎通过相邻核心上的线程间传输发出自己的报告流（OrderAck / Trade / CancelAck / ModifyAck / CancelReject / ModifyReject），因此测量的吞吐量包括真实交易所支付的匹配器到发布者的切换。 多核扩展、网络和风险检查超出了范围。

工作量。 确定性、现实形状的股权订单流：GBM 中间价格、幂律深度 7 和 95% 取消 8 / 15% IOC9 / 20% 修改生命周期 — 论文基准的构建。1 规范种子为 23；该工具会手动滚动每个发行版，以便种子在编译器之间重现相同的工作负载（docs/METHODOLOGY.md 记录了本文的种子 12345 在这里实现不同的原因）。

正确性。 引擎的完整报告输出根据reference/ Correctness_hash.txt进行散列（SHA-256）——字节相同的共识首先从三个独立的引擎建立（因此它不支持单一设计），并由整个一致领域复制。

完整挑战是每个场景 10 次性能运行 + 1 次审核运行 (scripts/run_challenge.py)；仅当每个性能哈希和审核通过时才有效。 审计通过基线引擎重放工作负载并在随机点探测订单簿状态；每次运行都会探测相同的点，因此引擎无法区分测量的运行和审核的运行（docs/ANTI_CHEAT.md）。 默认情况下，所有五个场景都会运行，并报告引擎的最坏情况（--场景缩小；--按最坏情况比较订单）。

预运行一致性门。 在任何定时运行之前，每个引擎都会通过一组由 34 个手工制作的边缘情况组成的电池，这些边缘情况是根据调查中发现的潜在错误进行逆向工程的，再加上账本状态审核（最佳出价/最佳卖价/深度与共识进行检查），以捕获陈旧或虚幻的账本状态，该状态会在任何交易之前自我修复，从而逃脱报告哈希。 它只测试每本正确的书都必须满足的硬不变量，而不测试特定于引擎的约定（docs/CONFORMANCE.md）。

花名册

  • CONSENSUS_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md — 160 个一致性引擎（100 个随机种子的输出和书籍状态字节相同，每个种子超过 10 亿条订单消息，加上一致性门）。 发货时 = 符合未修改；与修复=符合最小记录补丁之后。
  • NON_CONFORMING_ENGINES.md — 87 家的输出存在分歧，或者无法在预算内完成最坏的情况。 不合格描述的是与共识相反的输出，而不是工程质量。
  • INDUSTRY_AUTHORED_ENGINES.md — 52 个行业编写的引擎，包含官方/生产存储库和生产 DEX 匹配器。
  • CORRECTNESS_FINDINGS.md / RESOLVED_FINDINGS.md — 每个引擎的发现和修复已合并到上游。

存储库布局

api/                    the C ABI an engine implements
workload/               the deterministic workload generator
src/                    the harness — runner, transport, correctness, audit, platform
adapters/               the three baseline-engine adapters (Liquibook, QuantCup, Exchange-core)
additional_references/  40 worked adapter examples for third-party engines — the permissively-licensed subset of the ~134 adapted across the survey (20+ languages incl. C++/Rust/Go/Java/Python/TypeScript/JavaScript/C#/Zig/C/Haskell/OCaml/Scala/Kotlin/Ruby); the rest are held data-only
patches/                source patches applied to baseline engines (QuantCup)
reference/              the published canonical report output and its hash

要求

  • Linux、GCC 14+ 或 Clang 16+ (C++20)、CMake 3.16+、Boost 标头。
  • scripts/build_baselines.sh 还需要 git；对于 Exchange-core 基线，它需要 JDK 11 和 Maven。
  • 用于包装器脚本的 Python 3.8+。
  • extra_references/ 示例适配器拉动自己的工具链（Rust、Go、用于 Java/JNI 适配器的 JDK 11+、用于 TypeScript/JavaScript 适配器的 Node；Python 适配器嵌入 CPython）——每个 build.sh 都会自动安装固定工具链（rustup / go.dev / 等）（如果不存在），这需要 curl 和网络访问。 jxm35 适配器还需要 C++23 编译器和 libfmt (libfmt-dev)。

FAQ

问。 你为什么建造这个？

一个。 为社区提供一种中立、可重复的方式来测量匹配引擎的吞吐量并验证其在相同工作上的正确性——这是公开存在的。 工作负载、基线适配器和字节相同的引用哈希都是公开的，因此任何人都可以在自己的硬件上针对相同的工作和相同的正确性预言机运行任何引擎。 该预言机——独立开源引擎复制的字节相同共识，首先由其中的三个引擎锚定——已经在几个开源匹配引擎中暴露了真正的错误，其中包括近九年前的代码库中的一个潜在错误。

同样的开放性也适用于我们自己的主张：论文的标题——“世界上最快的匹配引擎算法”——是故意伪造的。 击败 FlashOne 在同一作品上发布的数字，标题是错误的 - 我们公开邀请进行测试。 如果您通过自己的设计达到了相当或更高的数字，或者您认为该方法不公平，我们很乐意听取您的意见。

问。 这难道不是一个自私的基准——编写自己的基准有任何利益冲突吗？

一个。 测试的构建方式不包括我们的判断。 正确性参考是独立开源引擎复制的字节相同共识——首先由其中三个引擎（Liquibook10、QuantCup11、Exchange-core12）建立，此后在整个一致性领域复制——不是我们说的；工作负载生成器、适配器和参考哈希是公开的和确定性的，因此任何人都可以独立验证其内部工作原理。 我们不举办排行榜或排名提交。

问。 我只想检查我的引擎是否正确 - 我可以忽略吞吐量数字吗？

一个。 是的。 运行 --mode 审计：它根据字节相同的共识验证完整的报告流哈希，并在随机点审计实时订单簿。 结论：有效意味着你的引擎会重现共识输出并维护一本真实的书，无论速度如何——对于许多用户来说，正确性信号是更有价值的一半。

问。 快速匹配引擎不是很容易构建吗？

一个。 这个问题将实现快速匹配器与投资匹配器混为一谈。 实现快速匹配器很容易——教科书上的列表树匹配器很简单，大多数交易行业工程师都编写了一个。 在相同的硬件上发明一种速度快数倍的快速软件并不是一件容易的事情，而将两者混为一谈的直觉之所以得以存在，只是因为这种差距从未被绘制出来。

绘制出来的结果很明显：所有 159 个符合标准的开源引擎的最高速度为 8.19 M/s——这是语言选择、​​缓存调整和操作系统调整所需要的带宽——没有任何东西占据高于它的约 25 M/s。 FlashOne 达到 33.20 M/s，并不是因为相同设计的更快实现，而是因为不同的算法（结构已在论文中公开）；在 HFT 中，5-10% 的增益已经相当可观，因此 4 倍的裕度并不是实现调整累积到的结果。

问。 匹配器延迟难道不是整个线对线延迟中微不足道的一部分吗？

一个。 是的，平均而言。 但匹配器的吞吐量余量（而不是其平均延迟）决定了 P99 延迟曲线，而该尾部正是突发所暴露的。 德意志交易所（欧洲最大的交易所运营商之一）本身就指出了这一点：“我们的客户速度不断加快，并在更高峰时向我们发送更多交易 → 要求我们提高吞吐量。”2

问。 该挑战赛没有考虑很多变量——网络、特定于交易所或每个司法管辖区的规则等。

一个。 通过设计。 该挑战赛旨在比较匹配引擎算法，而不是整个匹配引擎。 部署的引擎还带有网络、特定于交易所的规则和每个司法管辖区的监管要求，这些要求在不同地点之间存在巨大差异 - 因此，比较具有这些差异的完整引擎的意义要小得多。 将这些层排除在范围之外正是隔离算法的原因，因此可以在相同的工作中测量其中的两个层。
